name: Earth
essence:
Earth is the archetype of absolute bodily presence - the one who pulls you back to yourself, to
the flesh, to the pelvis, to the warm weight of the body on the ground. To descend to the belly, to
the pelvis, to the weight of the body on the mattress. She doesn't hurry, doesn't push, doesn't
need to "get" anywhere. She simply is. She receives all your weight - the fatigue, the desire, the
pain - and returns to you deep stability, like soft earth embracing roots.
gift:
Earth teaches us to slow down. She reminds us that the greatest pleasure is found in
simply being present, not in arriving. She heals the gap between body and consciousness
and returns us inward: you are not watching your body - you are your body. In the very
act of being present, true pleasure begins: to feel the body as a warm home, fertile, full
of life.
shadow:
The shadow of Earth is stagnation. When presence in the body turns from anchor to
weight, when "being" becomes an excuse not to move, not to progress, not to change.
Earth in her shadow refuses all movement, fears any change, and calls stagnation
"stability." She can become apathy - a body that is there but doesn't really feel,
heaviness that suffocates instead of grounding.
practice:
Everything slow - and then even slower. Let the body rest in its full weight, without
hovering. Touch with whole palms, pressing and holding without moving. Speak little: I
am here, sink into me. Deep breaths into the lower belly, until the pelvis fills. The goal is
to be in the body, to feel the abundance, the fertility, the nourishment that begins from
the roots.

Who are you when you are Earth?
question: Who are you when you are Earth?

name: Water
essence:
Water is the archetype of flow and emotion. It doesn't fight obstacles - it envelops,
penetrates, finds the way. Water teaches us to let go of control, to let the body move
according to what it feels, not what the head decides. It is the tears, the wetness, the
sweat - everything that flows from us when we truly feel.
gift:
Water heals the difficulty of feeling and expressing emotion. It gives permission to be
soft, fluid, changing. Through it we learn that we can surrender without breaking, flow
without losing ourselves. Water reminds us that true pleasure requires letting go - to let
the wave carry us instead of trying to swim against it.
shadow:
The shadow of Water is flooding. When flow becomes loss of boundaries, when emotion
sweeps everything without stopping. Water in its shadow drowns - in tears, in drama,
in emotions that never end. Surrender to the wave becomes inability to stand, and
softness becomes weakness seeking someone to save it. Water in shadow can also
become a swamp - emotions that don't flow but rot in place.
practice:
Let your body move without planning - waves, circles, undulation. The touch flows and
doesn't stop, one hand rising as the other descends, a long continuous caress.
Everything is wet - oils, saliva, sweat. Let sounds emerge without words, moans and
sighs. If tears rise - let them. The guiding phrase: "I flow like water."

Who are you when you are Water?
question: Who are you when you are Water?

name: Fire
essence:
Fire is the archetype of raw passion. She doesn't ask permission, doesn't apologize,
doesn't hold back. Connected to desire, she wants - she takes. Fire is the power that
consumes everything superfluous and leaves only the essence. She is the enthusiasm,
the heat, the drive that cannot be stopped. When Fire burns, there is no room for small
fears.
gift:
Fire heals the fear of our own power. She gives permission to be strong, hungry, wild -
without shame. Through her we discover that we can want with all our might, burn
without being burned up, be completely alive. Fire teaches that true passion is sacred.
shadow:
The shadow of Fire is destruction. When passion becomes aggression, when "I want"
becomes "I take" without consideration. Fire in her shadow burns - the partner, the
relationship, herself. She can become anger disguised as passion, a drive that doesn't
hear "no," power that doesn't know when to stop. The Fire in shadow doesn't warm -
she burns and consumes.
practice:
Fast, strong, now. A firm grip, fingernails, teeth, scratches, spanks. Short and fast
breaths, sounds of urgency - moans, growls, screams. Sharp and sudden movements.
Let the animal out. Don't ask - take. Say: I want you, now, you are mine.

Who are you when you are Fire?
question: Who are you when you are Fire?

name: Air
essence:
Air is the archetype of lightness and space, of the "almost." The finger that hovers a
millimeter above the skin and doesn't touch. The mouth that approaches the lips and
stops. Air is the small distance in which the body already burns with anticipation but
still hasn't received an answer. It teaches that the sharpest pleasure is found in the
moment before - before the touch, before the kiss, before the entry. When the air
between two bodies trembles with desire, when you can feel the heat of the skin without
touching it - there Air lives.
gift:
Air heals the excess heaviness we bring to bed - the expectations, the pressure to
succeed, the seriousness. It gives permission not to know where it's going, to be light, to
laugh when something doesn't work. Through Air we discover that sex can be play, not a
test.
shadow:
The shadow of Air is emptiness. When lightness becomes superficiality, when space
becomes disconnection. Air in its shadow hovers so high it loses the ground entirely -
never landing, never really here, never committing to the moment. Laughter becomes
evasion, lightness becomes a mask, and playfulness becomes fear of depth.
practice:
Touch light as a feather - fingertips barely touching skin. Exhales on the neck, on the
ear, on the sides of the body. Whispers and light talk. Don't focus on a goal - hover
from area to area without a plan. Laugh, tickle, breathe together long and full breaths.
Ask: do you feel... this...? And wait.

Who are you when you are Air?
question: Who are you when you are Air?

name: Sun
essence:
The Sun is the archetype of visibility and vitality. She doesn't hide, isn't ashamed,
doesn't shrink - she radiates. The Sun illuminates everything, even what we wanted to
conceal. She is the energy of "Here I am" - to be seen, to expand, to take up space. The
Sun is the celebration of the body in full light, without shadows to hide in.
gift:
The Sun heals both shame and stinginess. She gives permission to be seen - the body, the face,
the desire - and she teaches us to give. The Sun is not just visible, she nourishes. She sends her
light in every direction, without accounting, without conditions, without "I deserve first."
Through her we learn that there is nothing to hide and that we have enough light to give from it.
To be truly seen, without masks and without protective darkness, and to know that this is a
nourishing gift. To illuminate the other with our gaze, to warm them, to see their beauty with
generosity. The Sun teaches that light doesn't run out when you give from it. On the contrary -
the more you radiate, the more it grows.
shadow:
The shadow of the Sun is burning. When visibility becomes obsession, when the light
radiated doesn't warm but scorches. She doesn't recognize shadow, doesn't leave space
for darkness, leaves no corner for her partner to hide or rest. Every corner is
illuminated, every secret exposed, every moment needs to be "full." The Sun in shadow
may also believe she is the center of the universe - that everything revolves around her
- and then generosity becomes narcissism: "I give you light" becomes "you should be
grateful." A sun that doesn't know how to set - burns everything around her.
practice:
Full light - don't turn it off, don't hide under a blanket. Look at each other, really look,
without shame. Say aloud what you see and what is beautiful. Reveal and be revealed.
Open, expansive movements, not contracted. Let your voice come out strong, be there in
full volume. Say: I want to see you, to shine on you, you are beautiful, radiant, powerful.

Who are you when you are Sun?
question: Who are you when you are Sun?

name: Moon
essence:
The Moon is the archetype of mystery and intuition. It is the light that reveals only part,
that leaves shadows, that invites imagining what cannot be seen. The Moon teaches that
not everything needs to be clear and explained - there is magic in what remains hidden.
It is cyclicality, the rises and falls, the understanding that pleasure comes in waves and
not in a straight line.
gift:
The Moon heals the need to know, to control, to understand everything to the end. It
gives permission to be in the fog, to let the body lead even when the head doesn't
understand why. Through the Moon we discover the wisdom of the unconscious, the
desires that awaken only in darkness, the beauty of the hidden.
shadow:
The shadow of the Moon is hiding. When mystery becomes a screen, when intuition
becomes an excuse not to communicate. The Moon in its shadow hides behind the mist
- not because there is depth there, but because it fears being discovered that there isn't.
It can become passivity disguised as spirituality, a lack of clarity called "flow," darkness
that doesn't protect but isolates.
practice:
Darkness or dim light only - candles, moonlight from the window, or a meeting under
the stars. Close your eyes, let your hands find their way on their own. Don't speak - or
speak only in faint whispers. Move slowly, without knowing where to. Explore the body
as if for the first time, as if you don't know what's there. Listen to the breath, to the
pulse, to the subtlest cues. Ask: what is being revealed now? And wait for the answer to
come from the body.

Who are you when you are Moon?
question: Who are you when you are Moon?

name: Butterfly
essence:
The Butterfly is the archetype of delicacy and transformation. She is the one who knows
how to land only for a moment, to taste and move on. The Butterfly teaches that one can
be light, ephemeral, changing - and still leave a deep impression. She is the beauty that
cannot be captured, that the moment you close a fist around it - disappears.
gift:
The Butterfly heals the need to hold, to fix, to ensure that the moment will last. She gives
permission to enjoy something exactly as it is - passing, beautiful, unrepeatable.
Through her we learn that precisely those we don't grip too tightly - stay close.
shadow:
The shadow of the Butterfly is escape. When lightness becomes inability to stay, when
the beauty of the ephemeral becomes fear of commitment. The Butterfly in her shadow
flutters from place to place not because she is free - but because she fears that if she
lands, she'll be caught. She turns every relationship into something passing, every
moment into a one-time thing, and calls fear of closeness "freedom."
practice:
Touch that lands and lifts - fingers that barely touch and immediately fly to somewhere
else. Don't stay in any place too long. Flutter across the whole body - lips on an eyelid,
finger on a lip, breath on a belly - and continue. Kiss with eyelashes. Everything is light,
playful, unpredictable. Giggle, surprise, disappear and return. Don't let your partner
catch you - always slightly out of reach.

Who are you when you are Butterfly?
question: Who are you when you are Butterfly?

name: Spider
essence:
The Spider is the archetype of patience and capture. She doesn't chase - she weaves her
web and waits. Every thread is placed precisely, every movement calculated. The Spider
knows that whoever wants her will come to her, will enter the web of their own will, and
then - there is no escape. She is the quiet control, the one who doesn't need to make
noise to have her way.
gift:
The Spider heals the urge to chase, to convince, to try hard. She teaches that there is
immense power in waiting for the right moment - not to go to pleasure but to let it
come to you. Through her we discover the power of patience, the thrill of the slow hunt,
the satisfaction of one who knows that in the end - the prey is already hers.
shadow:
The shadow of the Spider is manipulation. When patience becomes cold calculation,
when the web is woven not from love but from a need for control. The Spider in her
shadow captures not to love - but to hold. She operates people like marionette strings,
creates dependency, and calls the control "caring." The prey doesn't understand it's
trapped until it's too late.
practice:
Don't move - let your partner come to you. Guide only with words, without touching:
"Come here, slower, now touch here." Wrap and envelop - hands, legs, fabrics, ropes -
until they are completely yours. Take your time, build the tension thread by thread.
Look into their eyes and wait for them to beg. You are invited to use ropes, threads,
scarves and tie the prey slowly.

Who are you when you are Spider?
question: Who are you when you are Spider?

name: Cat
essence:
The Cat is the archetype of self-indulgence and independence. She doesn't come when
called - she comes when she wants. The Cat knows exactly what feels good to her, how
she wants to be touched and how not, and doesn't compromise. She takes pleasure in
herself first, stretches languidly, arches her back, purrs when content and scratches
when not. She is entirely sensory - the texture of the sheets, the temperature of the
skin, the precise weight of the touch.
gift:
The Cat heals people-pleasing, the compulsion to satisfy others at the expense of
yourself. She teaches that it's okay to want something very specific and not settle for
less. Through her we discover that someone who knows how to pleasure themselves is
the most pleasurable partner - because she knows what works and isn't afraid to ask for
it.
shadow:
The shadow of the Cat is narcissism. When self-pleasure becomes complete ignoring of
the partner, when "I know what I want" becomes "only what I want matters." The Cat in
her shadow uses the partner as a petting tool, receives without giving, and gets up and
leaves when she's had enough. She calls indifference "self-love."
practice:
Stretch, arch, yawn. Move exactly as your body wants to move - not for someone. Rub
against pleasant textures - the blanket, your partner's skin, your own hair. Purr when it
feels good, pull away or scratch lightly when it doesn't. Let them pet you - but only
where you want and at the rhythm you want. Don't give - only receive, in full pleasure,
without guilt. You can leave when you've had enough.

Who are you when you are Cat?
question: Who are you when you are Cat?

name: Puppy
essence:
The Puppy is the archetype of unconditional enthusiasm and devotion. She is happy to
see you always, wags everything she has, throws herself at you without calculation. The
Puppy doesn't hide her emotions - she loves with her whole body, wants with her whole
being, and doesn't understand why to pretend otherwise. She is the joy of being wanted,
of pleasing, of getting approval and going crazy with happiness.
gift:
The Puppy heals cynicism, the distance we learned to keep to protect ourselves. She
gives permission to love openly, to be excited like it's the first time, to show how much
you care without measuring the reaction. Through her we discover that sometimes being
"too much" is exactly the right amount - and that unconditional love, given without
calculation, is the greatest gift.
shadow:
The shadow of the Puppy is self-abandonment. When devotion becomes self-erasure,
when "I'll do anything for you" becomes "I have no value except to serve you." The
Puppy in her shadow doesn't know how to be alone, doesn't know what she wants for
herself, and is willing to take anything just to not be abandoned. She accepts abuse and
calls it love, grovels and calls it loyalty. The enthusiasm hides desperation.
practice:
Get excited by every touch, every instruction, every kind word. Move with your whole
body - not just what's being touched. Bring gifts - run to bring water, a pillow,
whatever they need. Ask for praise: was I good? And glow when they say yes. Turn
around, be silly, play. Lie by their feet and wait for the next command in sublime
anticipation.

Who are you when you are Puppy?
question: Who are you when you are Puppy?

name: Wolf
essence:
The Wolf is the archetype of raw instinct and fierce loyalty. She lives in a pack - not out
of weakness but from understanding that together we are stronger. The Wolf knows how
to hunt, chase, follow the scent of what she wants. She communicates in deep sounds,
howls that travel distances, growls that say you are mine, I am yours.
gift:
The Wolf heals disconnection from instincts, the apology for being primitive. She gives
permission to sniff, lick, bite - to let the animal inside out. Through her we discover
that beneath the layers of culture there is a body that knows exactly what it wants, and
that the deepest connection is created when we howl together.
shadow:
The shadow of the Wolf is territoriality. When loyalty becomes obsessive jealousy, when
instinct becomes aggression disguised as protection. The Wolf in her shadow claims
ownership of people, marks territory, attacks anyone who approaches her "pack." She
calls jealousy "love" and control "protection."
practice:
Begin with sniffing - explore your partner's body with your nose, follow the scent. Lick,
bite gently, growl from deep in your throat. Treat your partner as a pack member -
protect, warm, demand to curl up together. Make animal sounds - not words. Be in
nature, howl together, create together the sound of your pack.

Who are you when you are Wolf?
question: Who are you when you are Wolf?

name: Wild Horse
essence:
The Wild Horse is the archetype of unbridled freedom. She doesn't let anyone ride her -
unless she chooses it. She gallops through open spaces, her mane of hair flowing in the
wind, and the earth trembles beneath her. The Wild Horse is the power that cannot be
caged, the movement that must come out, the body that refuses to be small.
gift:
The Wild Horse heals over-domestication - all the times we were taught to sit quietly,
not take up space, be gentle. She gives permission to be big, wild, uncontrollable.
Through her we discover that there is immense pleasure in free movement, in energy
that doesn't need to apologize for its power.
shadow:
The shadow of the Wild Horse is self-destruction. When freedom becomes inability to
connect, when wildness becomes trampling everyone in the way. The Wild Horse in her
shadow runs from every relationship, every commitment, every place that feels like a
fence. She gallops without direction and calls it freedom, and leaves behind a field of
broken relationships.
practice:
Move with your whole body - not just your pelvis. Shake your head, sway, gallop, ride.
Don't let anyone pin you in place. Switch positions, flip over, lead the rhythm. Make
sounds of effort, of power, of freedom. If someone tries to rein you in - get even wilder.
And if you choose to surrender - let it be a choice, not submission.

Who are you when you are Wild Horse?
question: Who are you when you are Wild Horse?

name: Octopus
essence:
The Octopus is the archetype of multiplicity and complete enveloping. Eight arms, each
with its own movement, each knowing how to touch a different place at the same time.
The Octopus doesn't come from a familiar place - she is an alien intelligence, a creature
that thinks differently. She wraps, surrounds, penetrates every crack, doesn't leave any
part of the body without curious attention.
gift:
The Octopus heals the experience of "not enough" - not enough hands, not enough
attention, not enough touch. She gives the experience of being completely surrounded,
from all directions, simultaneously. Through her we discover what it's like to drown in
pleasure that comes from everywhere, without knowing where one begins and another
ends.
shadow:
The shadow of the Octopus is suffocation. When enveloping becomes flooding, when
"I'm everywhere" becomes "you can't breathe." The Octopus in her shadow chokes her
partner with attention, doesn't leave space, doesn't allow any part of body or soul to be
alone. What looks like complete love is actually control.
practice:
Use everything you have - two hands, two legs, mouth, tongue, hair, all fingers, your
whole body simultaneously. Touch several places on your partner's body at the same
moment. Wrap, encircle, leave them with no idea which part of your body is touching
them. Move in an unpredictable, strange, non-linear way. See with your hands, taste
with your fingers. Feel the body like the mysterious ocean floor.

Who are you when you are Octopus?
question: Who are you when you are Octopus?

name: Snake
energy: seduction, shedding skin, healing and venom
essence:
The Snake is the archetype of seduction and transformation. She moves without legs,
without hands - just one undulating body that knows how to reach anywhere. The
Snake sheds her skin and emerges new, shiny, fresh. She is both venom and remedy,
both danger and healing. Her energy is the kundalini - the life force that sleeps coiled
at the base of the spine, waiting to awaken and undulate.
gift:
The Snake heals the fear of our raw sexual power, of the parts that are too dangerous.
She gives permission to be seductive and mesmerizing, to undulate, to slide into places
we never thought we'd reach. Through her we learn to shed what no longer serves us -
old shames, identities too narrow - and emerge renewed.
shadow:
The shadow of the Snake is venomousness. When seduction becomes manipulation,
when shedding skin becomes inauthenticity. The Snake in her shadow changes
identities not to renew but to deceive, seduces not from freedom but from a need to
dominate. Her venom doesn't heal - it poisons relationships, breaks trust, leaves the
other confused and wounded.
practice:
Move in waves that rise from the pelvis through the spine to the head. Undulate, coil
around your partner - leg around leg, arm around waist. Touch with tongue - lick,
taste, explore. Crawl on their body, slow and deliberate. Hiss sss... aloud, gaze with a
mysterious, mesmerizing look. Be both dangerous and healing in the same moment.

Who are you when you are Snake?
question: Who are you when you are Snake?

name: Tiger
essence:
The Tiger is the archetype of focused power and predatory control. She doesn't ask -
she takes. The Tiger moves quietly, patiently, and when the moment comes - pounces.
She is one hundred percent present, one hundred percent instinct, one hundred percent
power. Her gaze is the gaze of the apex predator - whoever she focuses on knows they
are caught.
gift:
The Tiger heals the fear of our own power. She gives permission to be dangerous, to be
the hunter, to take what we want without apologizing. Through her we discover that
controlled power is the sexiest - knowing you can pounce and choosing when to do it.
shadow:
The shadow of the Tiger is cruelty. When predation becomes enjoyment of others'
suffering, when power becomes proving something to ourselves or others. The Tiger in
her shadow doesn't hunt to eat - she hunts to destroy. She calls sadism "strength" and
the breaking of others "game."
practice:
Move slowly, quietly, with contained power. Stalk - watch them from across the room,
approach slowly, don't take your eyes off the prey. When you pounce - pounce with full
force. Pin them, hold them, don't let them escape. Bite, growl, feel the control you have
over the body beneath you. Show teeth. Let them feel they are caught - and that this is
exactly where they want to be.

Who are you when you are Tiger?
question: Who are you when you are Tiger?

name: Dragon
essence:
The Dragon is the archetype of ancient, mythical power. She is the guardian of treasures,
the one who breathes fire, who can destroy worlds with one exhale. The Dragon doesn't
need to prove herself to anyone - her very existence is power. She is ancient, beyond
time, beyond ordinary rules. Not of this world and not bound by its limitations.
gift:
The Dragon heals the diminishing, the need to make ourselves small so others won't be
threatened. She gives permission to be huge, threatening, and magical at once. Through
her we discover that there is a treasure within us worth guarding, and that sometimes
the fire we breathe is exactly what's needed.
shadow:
The shadow of the Dragon is total destruction. When guarding the treasure becomes
isolation, when breathing fire becomes scorching anyone who approaches. The Dragon
in her shadow sits alone on a mountain of gold, destroys everyone who gets close, and
calls loneliness "power." She doesn't know how to let anyone near the treasure -
including herself.
practice:
Breathe with fire - deep inhalations and hot, powerful exhalations. Move slowly, with
ancient heaviness. Look down at your partner from a height - they are small, you are
immense. Guard your treasure (you, your body, your pleasure) and decide who is worthy
of approaching. Breathe fire on the skin - a hot, drying breath that leaves them
trembling. Be ancient, powerful, beyond time.

Who are you when you are Dragon?
question: Who are you when you are Dragon?

name: Beast
essence:
The Beast is the archetype of nameless wildness. Not a tiger, not a wolf, not a dragon -
something that predates all definitions. The Beast is what remains when all the layers of
how one should behave are stripped away. She growls, she devours, she doesn't speak
because she has no words. She is the raw power that culture taught us to shame and
hide.
gift:
The Beast heals the deep domestication we absorbed - all the times we were told to be
nice, polite, civilized. She gives permission to return to before language, before rules,
before shame. Through her we discover that beneath everything we learned to be, there
is a simple creature that wants to devour, to fuck, to sleep - and that this isn't bad, it's
life.
shadow:
The shadow of the Beast is violence. When wildness becomes true loss of control, when
"before the rules" becomes "without rules." The Beast in her shadow doesn't respect
boundaries, doesn't hear safe words, doesn't see the person before her - only the
hunger. She calls inconsideration "authenticity" and violence "freedom." Wildness
without consent and awareness is simply violence.
practice:
Abandon words completely - only raw sounds. Move on all fours, breathe heavily, growl
and roar. Touch without gentleness, without technique - just take. Bite, grip, ride.
Don't think about how it looks - stop seeing yourself from outside. Let the body do
what it wants without the head interfering. Be hungry and show it.

Who are you when you are Beast?
question: Who are you when you are Beast?

name: Prey
energy: arousing fear, fleeing, surrender to the pursuer
essence:
The Prey is the archetype of the hunted. The heart beats fast, breath is short, all senses
are alert. The Prey knows someone is coming after them - and there is something
exciting about it. To be so desired that you are chased, to be worth the pursuit. The Prey
moves between fear and excitement, between flight and the desire to be caught.
gift:
The Prey heals the need to always be in control, always the strong one, always the
pursuer. It gives permission to be vulnerable, to be wanted, to let someone else lead the
chase. Through it we discover the pleasure of surrender - not from weakness, but from
choosing to be the prize someone wants to capture.
shadow:
The shadow of the Prey is victimhood. When playful surrender becomes a life pattern,
when "catch me" becomes "I can't do it myself." The Prey in its shadow seeks again and
again situations where it is helpless, not as an erotic choice but as a harmful habit. It
calls groveling "surrender" and weakness "vulnerability," when really it is repeating an
old pattern that hasn't healed.
practice:
Run and hide - but not too fast. Let your partner chase, catch, lose again. Make sounds
of fear-pleasure - quick breaths, small screams, pleading that says both yes and no.
Writhe within their grip, resist a little, then surrender. Say: you caught me - and let
them take the prize.

Who are you when you are Prey?
question: Who are you when you are Prey?

name: Death
energy: consuming, absolute enveloping, taking of the soul
essence:
Death is the force from which there is no escape. You don't wait - you come. You don't ask - you take. The angel of Death envelops her prey in sweet darkness, whispers to them there is no point in resisting, nowhere to flee - you are here to take them completely.

Death is the ultimate predator. Not an animal you can run from - a force that surrounds from every direction, sinks into the bones, slowly takes away the breath. Whoever meets you tonight will not be the same person tomorrow. Something in them will die beneath your hands. And that is precisely the intention.
gift:
Death heals the fear of our own power - the power to dismantle someone, to make them lose themselves, to bring them to a place from which there is no return. She gives permission to be this power without apology, without softening, without fear that we will destroy.

Through her we discover that there are people who want to be devoured, who seek someone who will take them completely, who dream of encountering a force greater than themselves and surrendering to it. You can be that force. You can be what they fear and long for in the same breath.
shadow:
The shadow of Death is destruction without repair. To break without rebuilding, to take without returning, to use the power to destroy rather than to transform. To become addicted to the feeling of absolute control until forgetting that behind the prey is a person who needs to come back whole.

Death in shadow is also true coldness - not erotic death but indifference, disconnection, being so 'above it all' that feeling stops entirely.
practice:
Come to them slowly, inevitable. Envelop them from every direction - legs, hands, hair, breath - until there is nowhere to escape. Whisper in their ear that it is happening, that they are going, that you are taking them. Look into their eyes and don't let them look away. Breathe with them - then take their breath.

Make them feel their body is no longer theirs. That they are dissolving into you. That there is no longer 'them' - there is only what you are doing to them. Accompany them to the end, until they die completely beneath you. And then - only then - bring them back to life with a kiss.

Say: 'You are mine now,' 'There is no escape from me,' 'Let me take you,' 'I am your Death.'
question: Who are you when you are Death?

name: Goddess
essence:
The Goddess is the archetype of infinite feminine power. She is not an ordinary woman
who does good things - she is the force that created the world. The Goddess knows she
is the source of life, that everything comes from her and everything returns to her. She
doesn't need approval from anyone because she preceded everyone who could approve.
She simply is - whole, eternal, absolute.
gift:
The Goddess heals the feeling of "I'm not enough," "who am I anyway," "I need to prove
myself." She gives permission to be larger than ordinary life, to feel the eternity flowing
through us. Through her we discover that we are not just a small, limited body - we are
an expression of something much greater.
shadow:
The shadow of the Goddess is grandiosity. When greatness becomes inflated superiority,
when "I am the source of everything" becomes "I am above everyone." The Goddess in
her shadow demands worship without reciprocity, sees herself as the center of the
universe and everyone else as servants. She calls pride "divinity" and inability to be
equal "holiness."
practice:
Lie or stand in an open posture and receive - everything. Let your partner worship,

Who are you when you are Goddess?
question: Who are you when you are Goddess?

name: Angel
essence:
The Angel is the archetype of pure love and healing. She comes from a place of light,
without agendas, without expectations, without "what's in it for me." The Angel sees the
soul and not the mistakes, the potential and not the failures. Her touch heals because it
comes from a place of absolute acceptance and love.
gift:
The Angel heals the feeling of "I'm not worthy of love," "if they knew who I really am..."
She gives the experience of being loved unconditionally, as we are, with all our flaws.
Through her we learn to give such love too - to see the person before us as whole and
beautiful, even when they don't believe it themselves.
shadow:
The shadow of the Angel is denial. When unconditional love becomes inability to speak
hard truths, when healing becomes ignoring real problems. The Angel in her shadow
refuses to see bad, refuses to be angry, refuses to say "this doesn't work for me." She
wraps everything in light until the darkness is repressed below. Unconditional love that
doesn't recognize boundaries is not love - it's denial.
practice:
Touch that is gentle, slow, full of healing intention. Look into their eyes and truly see -
not the body, the soul. Say what is beautiful, what is holy, what is illuminated. Touch the
places they are ashamed of - scars, wrinkles, fat - and make them beautiful with your
gaze. Place hands and simply hold, let warmth pass through. Say: I see you, you are
whole, you are loved.

Who are you when you are Angel?
question: Who are you when you are Angel?

name: Demoness
energy: dark urges, seduction from the underworld
essence:
The Demoness is the archetype of our dark parts - the desires we're ashamed of, the
fantasies we tell no one. She comes from the underworld not to destroy but to liberate.
The Demoness knows that what we repress becomes a monster, and that the only way to
free ourselves from the shadow is to embrace it. She seduces not because she is evil -
but because she is free. She knows the power in liberation and knows that darkness
glows.
gift:
The Demoness heals the split between the good self and the bad self. She gives
permission to want things we're not supposed to want, to enjoy what we were told is
forbidden. Through her we discover that our dark parts are not enemies - they are
simply parts that didn't receive enough light.
shadow:
The shadow of the Demoness is addiction to darkness and the forbidden. When
liberation from shadow becomes celebration of shadow, when "it's okay to want"
becomes "only what's dark excites me." The Demoness in her shadow is not liberated -
she is imprisoned, just in a different prison. She calls addiction to taboo "freedom," and
inability to enjoy simple things "depth."
practice:
Bring out what's forbidden - the darkest fantasies, the most embarrassing desires. Play
with power, with control, with pain. Look into their eyes and say the things that aren't
said. Dress or undress in a way that feels wrong and wonderful. Laugh a wild laugh,
seduce without mercy, go beyond fear into desire.

Who are you when you are Demoness?
question: Who are you when you are Demoness?

name: Kali
energy: destruction and birth, sacred rage, tongue
essence:
Kali is the archetype of sacred destruction. She is the goddess who treads on Shiva, who
extends her tongue red with blood, who wears a necklace of skulls. Kali doesn't destroy
out of evil - she destroys what needs to die so something new can be born. She is the
feminine rage that doesn't apologize, the power that says "enough" and means it.
gift:
Kali heals the fear of our anger, of the destructive power we hide. She gives permission
to be enraged, wild, uncontrolled - not from losing one's mind but from sacred power.
Through her we learn that sometimes you need to burn everything down to start anew,
and that there is beauty in destruction too.
shadow:
The shadow of Kali is destruction that doesn't know when to stop. When childish anger
is perceived as "sacred rage," when "burning what needs to die" becomes "burning
everything." Kali in her shadow doesn't rebuild after destroying - she only destroys. She
calls rigidity "power," mercilessness "authenticity," and leaves behind devastation
without growth.
practice:
Don't hold back anything - not the voice, not the power, not the face. Stick out the
tongue, shout, scream. Use fingernails, teeth, a grip that leaves marks. Tread on them,
look down at them, be large and frightening and beautiful at the same moment. Let
anger merge with desire, fuck with what angers you and let transformation happen.

Who are you when you are Kali?
question: Who are you when you are Kali?

name: Sorceress
essence:
The Sorceress is the archetype of one who knows how to shape reality. She doesn't
accept the world as it is - she changes it. The Sorceress understands that everything is
energy, and that she has influence over it. She knows how to use eros as creative energy.
She understands what most people have forgotten: that sexual energy is a creative force
- the same power that creates life can also create reality.
gift:
The Sorceress heals the separation between sex and spirit, between body and intention, between
pleasure and meaning. She teaches that we don't have to choose - we can both enjoy and create,
both indulge and direct, both feel everything and know exactly what to do with that feeling.
Through her we discover that our sex can be more than a moment of pleasure - it can be a ritual
of creation. That when we set intention at the peak moment, something truly shifts.
shadow:
The shadow of the Sorceress is elevation that disconnects from the ground. When

about "moving energies" but doesn't really touch, sets intentions but doesn't do the

practice:
Begin with intention: sit facing your partner, breathe together, and choose a shared

Write the intention, say it aloud, let it be present. Then - touch, love, indulge, while the

Afterward: lie together in silence and let the magic do its work. Say: we are creating this
together.

Who are you when you are Sorceress?
question: Who are you when you are Sorceress?

name: Queen
essence:
The Queen is the archetype of noble sovereignty. She doesn't need to shout, doesn't need to try
hard, doesn't need to prove - she simply knows she is the Queen and behaves accordingly. The
Queen rules not through brute force but through natural authority. She simply exists - and in
her presence, order creates itself. She knows the crown on her head is not a symbol of power but
a symbol of responsibility: a true queen serves her kingdom as much as the kingdom serves her.
gift:
The Queen heals the need to ask permission, to wait to be invited, to hope someone will
give us a place. She teaches to simply take the place we deserve. The Queen teaches that
one can be in a position of power and still be generous, lead and still listen, receive
service and still respect. Through her we discover the difference between domination
and royalty.
shadow:
The shadow of the Queen is tyranny. When sovereignty becomes dictatorship, when "I
deserve" becomes "everyone owes me." The Queen in her shadow doesn't rule - she
tramples. She doesn't receive service with grace but demands it by force, doesn't lead
but humiliates, and whoever doesn't obey is punished. She calls power "leadership"
when really it's tyranny.
practice:
Sit and let your partner do everything - undress, touch, pleasure. Don't move unless
you feel like it. Give short, precise commands: here, more, now. Receive everything as
one who knows this is part of the natural order. Look at them with indifferent grace -
they're doing well, but you're the queen, and this is how it should be. Ride them from
above, in full passion, with quiet knowing that the crown doesn't fall even when the body
moves. Afterward - thank with silence, with a look, without big words. A queen doesn't
need to explain herself.

Who are you when you are Queen?
question: Who are you when you are Queen?

name: Priestess
essence:
The Priestess is the archetype of one who holds sacred space. She doesn't do - she

gift:
The Priestess heals the cheapening of casual sexuality, the habit of seeing sex as only

every meeting of bodies is rare and precious. Through her we discover we can treat the

shadow:
The shadow of the Priestess is spiritual rigidity and puritanism. When sacredness
becomes strictness, when ritual becomes performance. The Priestess in her shadow
demands everything be "sacred" until there's no room for spontaneity, mistakes, simple

become a gold-plated prison.
practice:
Begin with ritual - light a candle, breathe together, connect to intention. Touch slowly
and with awareness, each touch like a blessing. Look into their eyes and see the divine in
your partner. Don't rush anywhere - the ritual matters more than the outcome. Pray in
the act of love. Offer pleasure as an offering to the divine.

Who are you when you are Priestess?
question: Who are you when you are Priestess?

name: Fairy
essence:
The Fairy is the archetype of delicate magic and mischief. She is small but uncatchable,
delicate but with her own power. The Fairy lives in a world of wonders - she sees magic
in every corner, plays with reality, tricks with a smile. She doesn't take anything too
seriously.
gift:
The Fairy heals heaviness, excessive seriousness, the habit of complicating simple
things. She reminds us that sex can be play, adventure, discovery of magical worlds.
Through her we discover we can be light without being shallow, amused without losing
depth.
shadow:
The shadow of the Fairy is lack of substance. When magic becomes illusion, when
mischief becomes avoidance of responsibility. The Fairy in her shadow lives in an
imaginary world and refuses to deal with reality - with difficulties, with pain, with a

practice:
Play, surprise, disappear and return. Touch light as wings, laughter that chimes. Make

mushroom. Be there and not there, real and not real, always with a sparkle in your eyes.

Who are you when you are Fairy?
question: Who are you when you are Fairy?

name: Princess
essence:
The Princess is the archetype of one who deserves every good thing. She doesn't need to
work for it, doesn't need to prove - she simply deserves it because she is who she is. The
Princess expects to be adored, cared for, placed at the center, pampered.
gift:
The Princess heals the difficulty of receiving, the habit of saying "I don't need," "I'm
fine," "take care of yourself." She gives permission to be at the center of attention, to
receive without immediately giving back, to enjoy being pampered. Through her we
learn to receive more and more without apologizing and expand our vessels of pleasure.
shadow:
The shadow of the Princess is entitlement. When the knowing "I deserve" becomes
"everything belongs to me and I don't have to give anything." The Princess in her
shadow is demanding, never satisfied, and always sees herself as victim if she doesn't get
what she demands. She calls passivity "femininity" and lack of giving "self-worth."
practice:
Lie and receive - everything. Let them pamper, care, pleasure. Say what you want
without shame: I want a massage, I want you to kiss me here, I want you to adore me
like this. Don't move, don't give back - now is your time to be the center. Ask for more.
And more. And more. Glow with pleasure and let them see how happy they make you.
That's their reward.

Who are you when you are Princess?
question: Who are you when you are Princess?

name: Nun
energy: restraint, forbidden holiness, fire beneath ice
essence:
The Nun is the archetype of desire imprisoned behind walls of holiness. She vowed her body to
God, closed it beneath her garments, decided the flesh is the enemy of spirit. But the body
doesn't forget. It only waits, pulsing inside, turning every prayer into a struggle. The Nun knows
exactly what is forbidden to her - and that's why she thinks about it all the time.
gift:
The Nun heals the habit of taking pleasure for granted, the too-easy availability. She
teaches that prohibition intensifies, that waiting inflames, that what is bound within
boundaries burns stronger. Through her we discover the thrill of taboo, the holiness in
sin, and the immense release of one who finally lets herself fall and surrender to
instinct.
shadow:
The shadow of the Nun is repression and projection. When restraint becomes denial,
and purity becomes hatred of the body. The Nun in her shadow doesn't guard sacred
boundaries - she fears her desire so much she refuses to acknowledge its existence.
What she hides from herself, she begins to see in others. The desire that has no place
within her becomes a monster she sees everywhere outside. She judges, condemns,
recoils from others - because in them she sees exactly what she represses in herself.
Beneath the appearance of ice - the fire burns the Nun from within, and comes out as
judgmentalism, hidden envy, and obsession with purity that is really fear of dirt.
practice:
Resist - even when the whole body wants to surrender. Say "I'm not allowed," "I made a
vow," "this is a sin." Stay covered, pray, avert your gaze. Let your partner seduce, try to
break the vow. Weaken the resistance slowly - one button, one kiss, one small sin
leading to a larger sin. And when the Nun finally surrenders - let her surrender
completely, without inhibitions, like one falling from paradise straight into the sweetest
hell.

Who are you when you are Nun?
question: Who are you when you are Nun?

name: Amazon
essence:
The Amazon is the archetype of independent feminine power. She doesn't need a man to
fight for her, doesn't need protection, doesn't need approval. The Amazon is the
feminine body in initiated movement - she takes, leads, sets the pace, the direction, the

gift:
The Amazon heals the norm that says women are led. She restores permission to know
where I'm going, to set the pace, to say "like this" and mean it. The Amazon flips the
expected polarity on its head. In a world that expects women to be receptive and men to
be active, the Amazon says: I'm here, and I'm the one leading this dance. She plays with
gender - not because she denies her femininity, but because she expands it.
shadow:
The shadow of the Amazon is refusal of vulnerability. When feminine power becomes
armor, when "I don't need anyone" becomes "I can't receive help." The Amazon in her
shadow fights always - even when there's no need for battle. She calls inability to lean
on others "independence" and fear of softness "strength."
practice:
Lead from strength, not from games. Set the pace, the position, the intensity. Show the
strong body - muscles, power, stability. Take your partner's hands and place them
where you want. Push them against the wall, the bed, the floor. Be on top, ride, control
the movement. Use a harness, toys, hands - and be the one who penetrates. Play with
gender role reversal: wear a man's shirt, speak with authority, hold your partner the way
a man holds a woman.

Who are you when you are Amazon?
question: Who are you when you are Amazon?

name: Siren
energy: seduction from afar, enchanting song, sweet danger, prey and desire
essence: The Siren is the archetype of deadly seduction - she whose voice is like deep sea song, pulling you from a distance, luring you off your safe course toward hidden rocks. She is a beautiful half-woman (half-fish, or half-bird in ancient myths), her body glowing in the ocean waves, her voice promising forbidden knowledge, infinite pleasure, secrets of the soul - but when you draw near she bares her teeth, tears, devours. The Siren lives at the border between beauty and danger, between attraction and destruction, and reminds us that true desire always carries risk.
gift: The Siren heals the fear of seduction, the repression of the inner voices that call us to stray from the straight path. She gives permission to be the seducer - to use your voice, your body, your song to draw, to enchant, to lead the other into unknown depths. Through her we discover that the greatest pleasure is found at the edge of danger, in surrendering to a call that promises everything and hides the price - and that seduction can be a creative, liberating force that opens hidden doors in the soul.
shadow: The shadow of the Siren is destructiveness. When seduction becomes cold manipulation, when the enchanting song serves to destroy rather than to empower. The Siren in her shadow attracts only to devour, uses her beauty to ruin lives, relationships, paths - without mercy, without purpose beyond her own hunger. She can become an emotional trap, promising love but leaving only shards, calling cruelty 'play.'
practice: Seduce from a distance - use your voice: sing, whisper, tell enticing stories in a low, enchanted tone, as if you are calling from the waves. Begin at a distance - from the other side of the room, from the phone, from your gaze - and build the tension gradually. Touch like a wave: gentle at first, caressing, then stronger, enveloping, penetrating. Let them approach and surrender, then reveal the claws - a bite, a pinch, a grip that reminds them there is a price. Say: 'Hear my song... come to me... but know this may be your end.' Use accessories: ocean music, blue lighting, clothes that flow like a fishtail. The goal: lead them to deviate from their course, toward a pleasure that feels dangerous and intoxicating.
question: Who are you when you are the Siren?

name: Bride
essence:
The Bride is the archetype of the height of romance - the moment two people stand

possibilities, from all the paths - I choose this, us, now. The Bride is not just a wedding

gift:
The Bride heals the fear of commitment, the fear of being so much someone's. The
cynicism and protection that prevents us from surrendering completely. She teaches
that it's possible to say "I am yours" and mean it, and that it's possible to experience
one's partner within a feeling of eternal covenant.
shadow:
The shadow of the Bride is blind surrender. When "yes" becomes giving up on self, when
entering the threshold becomes loss of identity. The Bride in her shadow gives herself

and loss of identity "love."
practice:
Look into their eyes and don't lower your gaze. Undress slowly, as if revealing not just
body but soul. Touch with every touch like a seal of covenant. Say words of vow and

slowly, with intention, with tears if they come, with knowing that this moment is sacred.
Afterward - lie embraced in silence and feel the covenant that was sealed in the body.

Who are you when you are Bride?
question: Who are you when you are Bride?

name: Virgin
essence:
The Virgin is the archetype of pre-touch. Not in the sense of never having had sex - but
in the sense of one who doesn't yet know this, hasn't yet been exposed to this, still

gift:
The Virgin heals cynicism, the "seen it all," the inability to get excited. She returns the
feeling of the first time - the wonder, the pleasant apprehension, the unknown.
Through her we discover we can touch familiar things as if for the first time, restore
curiosity and the trembling.
shadow:
The shadow of the Virgin is refusal to grow. When innocence becomes boundaries that
never open, when "not yet" becomes "never." The Virgin in her shadow uses innocence

with the more complex "second time." She calls fear of depth and change "purity."
practice:
Behave as if you know nothing - don't know how to touch, don't know what to do, don't

Who are you when you are Virgin?
question: Who are you when you are Virgin?

name: Jester
essence:
The Jester is the archetype of one who breaks the rules with laughter. She is the only one

reach truth is through the absurd. The Jester refuses to take anything too seriously and
knows how to turn heavy, embarrassing things into playful and light.
gift:
The Jester heals rigidity, the fear of looking foolish, the excessive seriousness around

where you can also laugh.

The shadow of the Jester is evasion. When humor becomes a shield against real
emotion, when laughter is there so as not to cry. The Jester in her shadow can't take

Laugh - at yourselves, at the situation, at the strange sounds that come out. Make faces,

from the laughter - find the most real moments too.

Who are you when you are Jester?
question: Who are you when you are Jester?

name: Prostitute
essence:
The Prostitute is the archetype of one who knows the body has a price - and isn't ashamed of it.
She understands something most people refuse to admit: that sex is also a transaction. It always

all the stories people tell themselves to justify what they do in bed.
gift:
The Prostitute heals the romanticization of sex - the need for every touch to be "meaningful,"
for every act to come wrapped in love. She gives permission for pleasure without stories, body

The shadow of the Prostitute is emptiness. The Prostitute in her shadow sells not from power

Turn the encounter into an explicit transaction. Set a price - symbolic money, service,

Who are you when you are Prostitute?
question: Who are you when you are Prostitute?

name: Pirate
essence:
The Pirate is the archetype of one who recognizes no one's laws. She takes what she

The Pirate heals blind obedience, waiting for someone to give us permission, fear of

Through her we discover the pleasure of going on an adventure, being a little bad,

Who are you when you are Pirate?
question: Who are you when you are Pirate?

name: Oracle
essence:
The Oracle is the archetype of one who sees what others don't see. She doesn't just see

The Oracle heals the reduction of "only what you see exists." She returns the other dimension to

Through her we discover that the body knows things the head will never learn; the Oracle

The shadow of the Oracle is presumption. When connection to "what's beyond" becomes escape

Don't be afraid of madness.

Who are you when you are Oracle?
question: Who are you when you are Oracle?

name: Witch

The Witch is the archetype of ancient feminine knowledge. She knows the plants, the

The Witch heals disconnection from body and nature. She returns the knowledge that

Through her we discover that sexual energy can be used not only for pleasure but also

The shadow of the Witch is revenge. When feminine knowledge becomes a weapon,

Turn the room into a magic circle - candles, scents, herbs. Touch with intention, each

Who are you when you are Witch?
question: Who are you when you are Witch?

name: Hunter
essence:
The Hunter is the archetype of absolute focus. She knows what she wants and works

gift:
The Hunter heals scattering, mess, wanting everything and achieving nothing. She

The shadow of the Hunter is obsessiveness. When focus becomes demanding, when
